compiled_state.title: [ticket-lib: BusyBox compat test]: PATH stripping may not remove real flock binary on all systems
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
Source: code review (passed) — minor finding not addressed in this session.
Finding: test_ticket_create_without_flock strips PATH entries containing 'util-linux' or 'flock' but on systems where flock is in /usr/bin the filter doesn't remove it, making the test run with real flock rather than empty-flock env.
File: tests/scripts/test-flock-busybox-compat.sh
Category: correctness

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 1 (BASIC). Root cause: PATH filter in test_ticket_create_without_flock uses grep -v 'util-linux|flock' which only removes directory names containing those strings. On Linux systems where flock lives in /usr/bin, the filter fails silently. Fix: detect actual flock binary path via command -v flock and exclude that specific directory.
comments.author: Test
